commands.review_summary:
The PR restricts MiniMax and Alibaba Coding Plan endpoint overrides to HTTPS while preserving bare-host HTTPS inference, and adds settings-reader regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows explicit override URLs with any scheme are accepted, and the affected fetchers attach Cookie or Authorization headers to the resolved provider requests.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-provided real behavior proof and maintainer acceptance of the compatibility/security policy tradeoff, not an automated repair.

target.title: Reject insecure credentialed endpoint overrides
target.body:
## Summary

Credentialed MiniMax and Alibaba Coding Plan requests allow endpoint overrides through environment settings. Before this change, explicit override URLs could use non-HTTPS schemes such as `http://...` while the fetchers still attached cookies and bearer tokens to the resulting requests.

This patch keeps the existing bare-host override behavior, but rejects explicit non-HTTPS schemes for these credentialed endpoints.

## Security impact

If a user or wrapper launches CodexBar with an insecure override value, provider cookies or bearer tokens can be sent over cleartext HTTP or another unsupported URL scheme. Those credentials are sufficient to query the provider-backed usage endpoints that CodexBar accesses on the user's behalf.

## Changes

- Reject non-HTTPS explicit URL overrides in `MiniMaxSettingsReader`.
- Reject non-HTTPS MiniMax host overrides before composing credentialed usage URLs.
- Reject non-HTTPS explicit URL and host overrides in Alibaba Coding Plan settings/fetcher helpers.
- Preserve the current convenience behavior where a bare host/path is interpreted as `https://...`.
- Add regression coverage for accepting bare/HTTPS values and rejecting `http://`/non-HTTPS override values.

## Verification

- `swift build --target CodexBarCore` ✅
- `swift test --filter 'MiniMaxSettingsReaderTests|AlibabaCodingPlanSettingsReaderTests'` could not complete in this checkout because the `CodexBarTests` target builds the macOS app dependency graph and fails in the transitive `KeyboardShortcuts` checkout with `PreviewsMacros` not found for SwiftUI `#Preview` macros. The new assertions are still included as regression coverage for normal CI/test environments.
target.author: hinotoi-agent
